sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Chest pain and difficulty swallowing.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports chest pain that started approximately three months ago, but resolved after an ER visit. The pain was sharp and felt like a heart attack. He denies any recent trauma. The patient also reports difficulty swallowing, which has also resolved since the initial episode. He experienced chest discomfort and possible heartburn around the time of the initial episode. He is currently experiencing some mild abdominal tenderness to light palpation. The patient reports attempting to lose weight and mentions a stressful job transition. He reports an EKG and blood tests were performed in the ER, which were normal. He previously sought care for esophagitis three months ago. He reports chest pain and difficulty swallowing.
Findings
Examination reveals mild tenderness to light palpation in the upper abdominal quadrants. The physical exam is otherwise unremarkable.
Assessment
The patient's symptoms suggest several potential etiologies, including GERD, dyspepsia, esophagitis, musculoskeletal etiology, and anxiety. Given the resolution of symptoms and lack of further episodes, further investigation is not immediately warranted. A history of heartburn is present, but the patient denies experiencing it.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: The doctor recommends monitoring for any additional symptoms and following up with the office if needed. The doctor recommends an egd if symptoms persist. The doctor recommends follow-up in four weeks if symptoms persist. The patient will be instructed to call the office if any new symptoms develop.
